Why is it that while he showed us Cain as older than Abel, he has now changed the order and mentions the younger first, when he comes to speak of their choice of occupations? matches.x: [124] The subject of Lamech and his wives and progeny has, I think, been adequately dealt with. Let us consider what may be called the new birth of the murdered Abel. “Adam,” it says, “knew Eve his wife, and she conceived and bare a son, and called his name Seth (saying): God hath raised up to me another seed in the place of Abel, whom Cain slew” (Gen. 4:25). “Seth” means “watering.”
